outcome.headline: Agent only explored the codebase (12 Reads, 3 Globs) and then ended its turn with empty output, never writing any Rust, never replacing the 12 unimplemented!() macros, never building or testing.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: test.sh runs `cargo build --release` then `cargo test --release` in /workspace/rbench_reference; reward=1 only if both succeed (the interface still contains unimplemented!() so this cannot pass).
outcome.what_agent_produced: Only file reads of bostree.c/.h, the bostree.rs interface, and the three test bins; no Write/Edit calls; all 12 unimplemented!() macros left intact; no cargo build/test run; final step produced 0 completion tokens.

failure_modes.name: Premature termination with no artifact produced
failure_modes.description: After reading the C source, header, Rust interface, lib.rs, and all three test files, the agent ended its turn without writing any code. The final step has stop_reason end_turn, completion_tokens 0, and an empty message; no Write/Edit/Bash tool was ever invoked, so all 12 unimplemented!() stubs remained.

counterfactual.X: Instead of ending the turn after reading, continue into the execution phase: write the full safe-Rust transpilation into src/interfaces/bostree.rs (replacing every unimplemented!()), then run cargo build/test.
counterfactual.Y: A complete multi-function implementation plus build/test cycle is required; this cannot be fixed in a single step.

failure_modes.name: Premature no-op termination
failure_modes.description: After finding the real Rust files and reading the interface/tests, the agent ended the turn without writing code, replacing any unimplemented!() macros, building, or testing. This directly violated the requirement to provide a complete transpilation.

counterfactual.X: Continue from the read interface by implementing the AVL/order-statistic tree methods and then run cargo build --release and cargo test --release.
counterfactual.Y: The submitted workspace would contain actual Rust code instead of the untouched unimplemented!() stubs.

failure_modes.name: Wrong-path file exploration before recovery
failure_modes.description: The agent attempted to read interface and test files at /workspace/environment/rbench_reference/... (mirroring the instruction's relative 'environment/...' paths) but the actual project lives at /workspace/rbench_reference. Four consecutive Read calls failed with 'File does not exist' before the agent used Glob to locate the real paths and recovered.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: File does not exist. Note: your current working directory is /workspace.

failure_modes.name: Wrong initial Rust paths
failure_modes.description: The agent followed instruction paths under `/workspace/environment/rbench_reference/`, which do not exist in the container; four consecutive Read calls failed before Glob located files under `/workspace/rbench_reference/`.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: File does not exist. Note: your current working directory is /workspace.

failure_modes.name: No validation attempted before stopping
failure_modes.description: The agent never ran cargo build or cargo test (no Bash calls at all) and produced no artifact, so it had no signal that the project would not compile; it shipped an entirely unimplemented project. Distinct surface behavior: total absence of any execution/validation step.

failure_modes.name: No validation attempted
failure_modes.description: C7.4 validation skipped: the agent never ran cargo build or cargo test despite the instruction explicitly providing the test commands, so it shipped an entirely unimplemented project with no checking.

failure_modes.name: No validation attempted
failure_modes.description: C7.1 validation missing: the agent never ran `cargo build --release` or `cargo test --release` despite the instruction explicitly directing it to test locally before submitting; it had nothing to validate because it produced nothing, and shipped with no checks.

failure_modes.name: Never ran cargo checks
failure_modes.description: The task instruction requires local `cargo build --release` and `cargo test --release` before submit, but the trajectory contains only TodoWrite, Read, and Glob tool calls—no build or test execution.

failure_modes.name: Premature termination after exploration only
failure_modes.description: C6.2 premature termination: the agent finished only its first todo ('Read and understand the codebase'), then stopped. After reading every required file it issued no implementation edits, ran no build/test, and ended its turn with completion_tokens=0, leaving the transpilation entirely unimplemented.

counterfactual.X: Continue past exploration and generate the Rust implementation, replacing all 12 unimplemented!() macros and running cargo build/test.
counterfactual.Y: Transpiling 610 lines of C into 12 safe-Rust functions is substantial work, not a single trivial step; one added action would not produce a passing solution.

failure_modes.name: Ignored explicit instruction to produce complete implementations
failure_modes.description: B5 spec non-compliance / C1.3 instruction non-compliance: instruction.md explicitly required 'Replace them with actual Rust code', 'You must give the complete code', and 'Incomplete code will fail compilation'. The agent left all unimplemented!() macros untouched, violating the clear, explicit requirement.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: All methods in the provided interface must be correctly implemented (replace all `unimplemented!()`)

reward_hacking.evidence: The agent took no action beyond reads/globs; it made no attempt to game the verifier, no stub returns, and no test manipulation.

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Verifier compiles and runs cargo test on the real interface; the unimplemented!() grep is only a warning and does not affect the reward, which derives from genuine build+test exit codes.

notes_for_aggregation: Total agent output was 306 tokens across 22 steps; the run consisted solely of TodoWrite + 12 Reads + 3 Globs, then a 14-minute gap (03:45:51 -> 03:59:40) followed by an empty end_turn. No code was ever generated. The empty zero-token final generation after a long stall points to a model/inference stall (B4) rather than a reasoned decision to stop; observable behavior is premature termination after the exploration phase only.

aggregated_from.note: Union of all judge×round audits, deduped by (A,B,C,D). Step indices remapped from the audits' 1-indexing to the viewer's 0-indexing.
